title: A Semistable Model for the Tower of Modular Cures
speaker: Jared Weinstein
abstract: The usual Katz-Mazur model for the modular curve $X(p^n)$ has horribly singular reduction. For large n there isn't any model of $X(p^n)$ which has good reduction, but after extending the base one can at least find a semistable model, which means that the special fiber only has normal crossings as singularities. We will reveal a new picture of the special fiber of a semistable model of the entire tower of modular curves. We will also indicate why this problem is important from the point of view of the local Langlands correspondence for $GL(2)$ .

proof_stages.label: Katz–Mazur model and supersingular crossings
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up the modular curves Y(Γ(p^n), Γ1(N)), recalls their Katz–Mazur integral model, and identifies the Igusa-curve components crossing at supersingular points as the places where the model must be altered.

proof_stages.label: Semistable models, dual graphs, and relative existence
proof_stages.summary: A semistable model is defined by normal-crossing special fiber and encoded by a dual graph of components, after which Deligne–Mumford existence and Coleman's relative finite-morphism theorem are recalled.

proof_stages.label: Cohomological payoff and known low-level cases
proof_stages.summary: Semistable reduction is motivated by the exact sequence expressing ℓ-adic H^1 in terms of graph and component cohomology, and the new tower result is placed beyond the known X0(p^rN) cases.

proof_stages.label: Localization to Lubin–Tate supersingular regions
proof_stages.summary: The global modular-curve problem is reduced to rigid supersingular regions M_n parameterizing deformations of a supersingular formal group, whose inverse-limit Lubin–Tate tower carries GL2(Qp), B×, and Weil-group symmetries and realizes local/Jacquet–Langlands correspondences in cohomology.

proof_stages.label: Coleman semistable-covering method
proof_stages.summary: The construction is recast in Coleman's language of wide-open coverings with annular pairwise intersections, no triple intersections, and nonsingular affinoid cores, data equivalent to a semistable formal model.

proof_stages.label: Equivariant semistable tower after deleting CM points
proof_stages.summary: The main construction produces semistable models for the M_n, after removing CM points and passing to a sufficiently large integral base, with finite transition maps and compatibility with the appropriate group actions along the tower.

proof_stages.label: Stable inseparability and the enhanced dual graph
proof_stages.summary: For any chain of irreducible components in the tower, the transition maps become purely inseparable at high level, so genera and ℓ-adic cohomology stabilize and one can form an infinite dual graph whose vertices are component chains labeled by stable curve classes.

proof_stages.label: Vertex cohomology, types, and the explicit graph
proof_stages.summary: The supercuspidal cohomology is rewritten as a direct sum over vertex curves and as induced representations from vertex stabilizers, matching Bushnell–Kutzko types, and the resulting graph is described as a tame Bruhat–Tits tree with wild Artin–Schreier branches whose finite-level models are obtained by quotienting.
